input.user_input: Summarize the main protections under the Electronic Communications Privacy Act relevant to enterprise SaaS providers.

actual:
The Electronic Communications Privacy Act (ECPA) of 1986 is a foundational US federal law governing the privacy of electronic communications. For enterprise Software as a Service (SaaS) providers, the most relevant component of ECPA is Title II, known as the **Stored Communications Act (SCA)**. 

Because enterprise SaaS providers host, process, and store data and communications on behalf of their business customers, they typically qualify under the SCA as either an **Electronic Communication Service (ECS)** (providing the ability to send/receive communications) or a **Remote Computing
